title: Tuberaria villosissima

Tuberaria villosissima is a flowering plant with hairy stems and leaves. It is a low-growing, shrub-like species that thrives in dry, rocky areas.

intro: Tuberaria villosissima is a Mediterranean plant species, also known as the hairy globular sea spurge, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tuberaria villosissima include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tuberaria villosissima is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to alleviate respiratory issues.
